data_in.description: Compiled EI mass spectral library object in R (e.g., result of read_lib or combined libraries via c() operator)
NIST ri.dat file (retention index database file from NIST installation)
NIST USER.DBU file (user database file from NIST installation)

data_out.description: EI library object with RI field populated for each compound based on median experimental RI from capillary columns, excluding records with standard deviation > 30

domain_knowledge: Kovats retention index (RI) is a standardized measure of compound retention time on gas chromatography columns, essential for EI library matching and compound identification.
Capillary GC columns are the standard platform for modern EI-MS experiments; Lee RI values from packed columns are excluded to ensure data homogeneity.
When multiple RI records exist for the same compound across different experiments, median RI is used to reduce noise; values with standard deviation exceeding 30 are rejected to exclude outliers.
The assign_ri function requires specification of column polarity (semi-polar, non-polar, or polar) to match RI data to the appropriate experimental conditions.

Verify script_runs: R script that calls assign_ri() to populate RI field in compiled EI library object completes without error
Verify field_present: resulting R object or MSP output file contains 'RI' field or equivalent retention index metadata field
Verify value_in_range or contains_substring: RI values in output are numeric and match expected format for Kovats retention indices (typically integer or decimal values in range ~500–3500)
expert_review: Confirm that RI values extracted from NIST ri.dat file are chemically and analytically plausible for the corresponding compounds (expert judgment of retention index correctness)
Confirm that RI assignment to library entries is chemically consistent — i.e., compounds are matched to correct RI values and not transposed or corrupted

cmd: library(mspcompiler); compiled_lib <- readRDS('{inputs.compiled_ei_library}'); ri_data <- extract_ri('{inputs.nist_ri_dat_file}', '{inputs.nist_user_dbu_file}'); result_lib <- assign_ri(compiled_lib, ri_data, polarity='semi-polar'); saveRDS(result_lib, 'task_003_output_library.rds'); cat('RI assignment complete. RI field populated:', !is.null(result_lib@RI), '\n')

inputs: Compiled EI mass spectral library object in R (e.g., result of read_lib or combined libraries via c() operator)
NIST ri.dat file (retention index database file from NIST installation)
NIST USER.DBU file (user database file from NIST installation)
methodology_summary: Load a compiled EI library object and NIST RI database files (ri.dat and USER.DBU) into R.
Parse NIST RI database files using extract_ri to retrieve experimental retention index records.
Filter RI records to retain only those from capillary GC columns; compute median RI for compounds with multiple records and discard records with standard deviation > 30.
Assign filtered RI values to the EI library object using assign_ri with semi-polar column polarity specification.
Validation: Verify that RI field is populated in the output library object and confirm presence of RI values in MSP output file.

title: Implement the NIST RI Extraction and Assignment Step using extract_ri and assign_ri

Synthesis grounding: the following tools/outputs were NOT found in the source paper and are inferred — verify before use: NIST, EI library object with RI field populated for each compound based on median experimental RI from capillary columns, excluding records with standard deviation > 30.
workflow_description: 1. Load a compiled EI library object (e.g., from read_lib or a combined EI library from multiple sources) into R. 2. Extract experimental Kovats RI data from NIST ri.dat and USER.DBU files using extract_ri, which parses the NIST RI database files. 3. Apply assign_ri to the compiled EI library, specifying polarity = "semi-polar" to filter RI records from capillary GC columns only, and discard RI values where standard deviation exceeds 30 or use median RI when multiple records exist for a single compound. 4. Verify that RI fields are populated in the resulting library object by inspecting the RI column in the output or writing the output to MSP format using write_EI_msp and confirming RI presence in the file.
